source: Invested by Aleph — Semiconductors, AI, global warming, geopolitics
summary: Artificial superintelligence (ASI) will arrive within 10 years; economic returns are unknowable but the trajectory is supportive
summary_extended: Baker makes an explicit 10-year prediction that artificial superintelligence (ASI) — defined as an AI system smarter than the smartest human in every discipline — will arrive by approximately 2034, and that the current trajectory of AI development supports this timeline. He identifies two primary risks to the current AI investment cycle: first, that the economic returns from ASI prove disappointing (no transformative breakthroughs like warp drives or cancer cures), and second, that edge AI — capable, locally-running models (characterized as '130 IQ AI on a phone') — disrupts the centralized data center buildout by decentralizing compute away from hyperscale infrastructure. The investment implication is that the current AI infrastructure boom is well-supported on a long-term basis, but investors must monitor whether edge AI commoditizes centralized compute demand. No specific equity is the subject of this thesis; it is a macro-level structural view on the trajectory of AI development rather than a company-specific call.
detail: Baker defines ASI as an AI smarter than the smartest human in every discipline. He believes current trajectory supports getting there. The two biggest risks to the current AI cycle are: (1) economic returns to ASI are lower than expected (no warp drives/cancer cures), and (2) edge AI (130 IQ AI running locally on a phone) disrupts the centralized data center boom via compute decentralization.
confidence_evidence: Baker states 'I think we will have artificial superintelligence in 10 years' as his explicit 10-year prediction
quote_fragment: we will have artificial superintelligence in 10 years

ticker_audit.reasoning: Baker's thesis is a macro view on ASI arrival and its risks — no specific claim is made about Nvidia's business, pricing power, or competitive position. NVDA is not the subject of or a direct beneficiary uniquely implied by this thesis; it bleeds in from adjacent AI infrastructure discussions.
Google is not specifically named or uniquely implied in this ASI timeline thesis. The thesis does not make a company-specific claim about Alphabet's positioning relative to ASI or edge AI disruption.
Microsoft is not the subject of or a direct beneficiary uniquely implied by this thesis. The macro ASI prediction does not single out MSFT as a specific winner or loser.
Amazon is not specifically implicated in this thesis. The ASI timeline prediction and its two identified risks do not make a company-specific claim about AWS or Amazon's positioning.
